limitations: Small sample size (122 users) — percentages are directional.
First AI command inferred from llm_usage.timestamp (no first_command_at column).
Token limits inferred from llm_daily_usage vs plan limits, not UX events.
NULL plan_id treated as Free (121/122 users historically).
CAC unavailable — user_acquisition and events tables empty.
Feedback quality requires manual review of sampled rows.
DAU buckets use sessions ∪ llm_usage; flow rates are 7-day average daily transitions.

cac_ltv.note: user_acquisition and events tables empty — provide marketing spend externally

definitions.current: Active today and at least one other day in the prior 7 days
definitions.reactivated: First day back after 7–29 days away
definitions.resurrected: First day back after 30+ days away
definitions.at_risk_wau: Inactive today, active on at least one of the prior 6 days
definitions.at_risk_mau: Inactive today and prior 6 days, active 7–29 days ago
definitions.dead: No activity in the last 30 days

kpi_rows.tooltip:
What it means: Share of all users who sent their first AI prompt within 24 hours of signing up. Higher means onboarding is working quickly.

Trend: Not enough daily snapshot history yet to show a trend.

Goals: Context: 176 on waitlist. Product Hunt launch may add 200–2000 signups in ~3 days — activation and conversion drive the 500 subscriber goal by Dec 31. Goal: 500 paid subscribers by Dec 31, 2026; May 2026 target: 17 (behind pace). You have 1 (0.2% of year-end goal). Gap to year-end: 499 subscribers.

key_insights.summary: Subscribers: 1/17 this month (500 by Dec 31). 77.9% of the base is dead. 25 users are at-risk (recoverable before 30d dead). Focus on at-risk WAU and resurrection levers.

items.title: Product Hunt in 3 days
items.detail: Launch May 27 ~3am ET may add 200–2000 users on top of 176 waitlist. Subscriber goal: 500 by Dec 31.
items.lever: Maximize 24h activation and limit-hitter → paid conversion before and during launch.

items.title: Subscriber goal gap
items.detail: 1 paid subscribers — May 2026 target 17 (16 behind). Year-end goal: 500 by Dec 31 (499 to go).
items.lever: Improve upgrade path at token limit; resurrect engaged free users.

items.title: Majority of users are dead
items.detail: 77.9% of users (95/122) have had no activity in 30+ days.
items.lever: Prioritize resurrection email flows and at-risk prevention (iWAURR).

items.title: Current users are slipping to at-risk
items.lever: Habit loops, return triggers, and session reminders.

items.title: More users dying than resurrecting
items.lever: Invest in dead → resurrected campaigns; test win-back offers.

items.title: Email send milestones not tracked yet
items.detail: 3 readiness milestones (welcome, NPS, PMF) pending cs_outreach_log. Product milestones (prompt, limit, training) are live in the matrix below.
items.lever: Deploy cs_outreach_log and wire CS agent dedup before launch email scale.

items.title: Low recovery from at-risk WAU
items.detail: Only 18.8% of at-risk WAU return to Current today.
items.lever: Test same-day or next-day re-engagement for users who missed one day.

items.title: Limit-hitters are not converting to paid
items.detail: Only 0.0% of users who hit token limits are on Plus.
items.lever: Improve upgrade UX at limit moment.

Goals: Context: 176 on waitlist. Product Hunt launch may add 200–2000 signups in ~3 days — activation and conversion drive the 500 subscriber goal by Dec 31.
metric_tooltips.churn_14d_pct:
What it means: Ever-active users with no activity in the last 14 days.

Trend: Not enough daily snapshot history yet to show a trend.

Goals: Goal: 4.5× DAU vs launch-week baseline (set after PH launch). Focus on activation before May 27 influx. Lower churn protects DAU and path to 500 subscribers.
metric_tooltips.flow_iMAURR:
What it means: At-risk MAU → Reactivated — average daily % of users making this transition over the last 7 days. Higher resurrection and iWAURR/iMAURR help win back at-risk and dead users.

Trend: Not enough daily snapshot history yet to show a trend.

Goals: Goal: 4.5× DAU vs launch-week baseline (set after PH launch). Focus on activation before May 27 influx. Reducing dead/at-risk users supports DAU and subscriber goals.
metric_tooltips.token_limit_hit_rate:
What it means: Share of users who hit the daily AI token cap at least once. Often signals power users who may upgrade.

Trend: Not enough daily snapshot history yet to show a trend.

Goals: Context: 176 on waitlist. Product Hunt launch may add 200–2000 signups in ~3 days — activation and conversion drive the 500 subscriber goal by Dec 31.
metric_tooltips.retention_d7:
What it means: Share of users who came back on day 7 after signup (session or AI usage). A leading indicator of whether new users stick.

Trend: Not enough daily snapshot history yet to show a trend.

Goals: Context: 176 on waitlist. Product Hunt launch may add 200–2000 signups in ~3 days — activation and conversion drive the 500 subscriber goal by Dec 31.
metric_tooltips.bucket_dead:
What it means: Dead users: No activity in the last 30 days

Trend: Not enough daily snapshot history yet to show a trend.

Goals: Goal: 4.5× DAU vs launch-week baseline (set after PH launch). Focus on activation before May 27 influx. Reducing dead/at-risk users supports DAU and subscriber goals.
metric_tooltips.churn_30d:
What it means: Ever-active users with no activity in the last 30 days.

Trend: Not enough daily snapshot history yet to show a trend.

Goals: Goal: 4.5× DAU vs launch-week baseline (set after PH launch). Focus on activation before May 27 influx. Lower churn protects DAU and path to 500 subscribers.

email_provider_capacity.estimation_note: v1 estimates: Beehiiv Phase 1 contacts; EmailOctopus Phase 2 conversion; fallback pool = MailerLite + OmniSend + Brevo (used when primary at cap); HubSpot = paid + company email.
